creator: koobs@FreeBSD.org
text:
@Churchers thanks for your submission.

All else being equal, it's almost always good to target:

1) Only one attachment with changes. Sometimes multiple versions of a patch are submitted. In your case there is a shar of the directory and a diff to svn

Attachment authors/submitters should 'obsolete' any that are no longer relevant or they dont want a FreeBSD committer to reference. You can do so by clicking Attachment Details -> Edit Details -> [X] Obsolete.

2) shars are only appropriate for "new" ports, unified diffs for everything else

So from here:

* obsolete the attachment that you don't want a committer to reference
* set maintainer-approval to "+" on the attachment that you do

My guess is it's attachment 163043, as attachment 163041 is a shar (should be a diff for updates) and is older than the former.
